pi_items.source: Web search + data file inspection. SPY is documented as the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust, the world's largest and most-traded ETF. Historical data file SPY.Last.txt contains verified OHLCV data for this instrument.
Web search for 'Exponential Moving Average EMA technical indicator'. EMA is documented as a standard technical analysis indicator that weights recent price data more heavily than older data.
Web search shows EMA with period 20 is a standard and widely-used setting. Day traders commonly use 8- and 20-day EMA periods. Period 20 weights the most recent price at 9.52%.

Web search for '2024 trading calendar'. January 16, 2024 was a regular NYSE trading day (Tuesday, following MLK Day holiday on January 15). Data file SPY.Last.txt contains entry: '20240116;475.26;476.61;473.06;474.93;85014900'

Web search for 'NinjaTrader 8 workspace directory location'. Official NinjaTrader documentation shows workspaces are stored at C:\Users\<username>\Documents\NinjaTrader 8\workspaces. The 'Docker' username is confirmed in setup scripts (setup_ninjatrader.ps1).

Code analysis. setup_ninjatrader.ps1 creates directory 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\NinjaTraderTasks'. verifier.py references this exact result_path at line 28. The path is consistent with the environment setup.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses SPY (SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust), the world's most-traded ETF tracking the S&P 500 index. The setup involves configuring an EMA (Exponential Moving Average) indicator with period 20, a standard technical analysis setting widely used by day traders. The replay date of January 16, 2024 is a valid trading day (Tuesday). Real historical OHLCV data for SPY covering this date is present in the data file (475.26 open, 476.61 high, 473.06 low, 474.93 close, 85,014,900 volume). NinjaTrader 8 workspace files are stored in the standard location C:\Users\Docker\Documents\NinjaTrader 8\workspaces. All metadata claims are grounded in verified technical documentation and actual data.
